sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) image, figure, from the root שָׂכָה, Ch. שְׂכָא to look at, to behold, Eze 8:12, מַשְׂכִּית חַדְרֵי “the chambers of images,” i.e. the walls of which were adorned with painted figures of idols, compare verses 10 Eze 8:10, 11 Eze 8:11. אֶבֶן מַשְׂכִּית Lev 26:1, and מַשְׂכִּיֹּות Num 33:52, stones adorned with superstitious or magical figures; Pro 25:11, תַּפּוּחֵי זָהָב בְּמַשְׂכִּיֹּות כֶּסֶף “apples of gold, adorned with figures of silver.” Others render, in silver baskets, so that מַשְׂכִּית would take its signification from שָׂכַךְ to plait.

(2) imagination, opinion, Psa 73:7 Pro 18:11.
